items.title: Slicing and safe downcasts: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CPP misconception? static_cast from Base* to Derived* checks the runtime object and returns null if the object is not Derived.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Use dynamic_cast for a checked polymorphic downcast; static_cast performs no runtime type check and is valid only when the programmer already proves the dynamic type.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Use dynamic_cast for a checked polymorphic downcast; static_cast performs no runtime type check and is valid only when the programmer already proves the dynamic type.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned CPP correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: Use dynamic_cast for a checked polymorphic downcast; static_cast performs no runtime type check and is valid only when the programmer already proves the dynamic type.
static_cast from Base* to Derived* checks the runtime object and returns null if the object is not Derived.

answer.explanation: Use dynamic_cast for a checked polymorphic downcast; static_cast performs no runtime type check and is valid only when the programmer already proves the dynamic type.

items.title: Slicing and safe downcasts: executable application
items.prompt: Pass a Dog through an Animal reference without slicing, use dynamic_cast to check the derived type, and print dog plus its age when the cast succeeds.

hints: Begin with the declared input and exact output contract, then implement the named language facet without relying on undefined behavior.

feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check failed; inspect input validation, boundary behavior, and the named language rule without exposing hidden cases.
